  code is extracted from Calendly's embed stylesheet: https://assets.calendly.com/assets/external/widget.css
*/

.calendly-inline-widget,
.calendly-inline-widget *,
.calendly-badge-widget,
.calendly-badge-widget *,
.calendly-overlay,
.calendly-overlay * {
    font-size:16px;
    line-height:1.2em
}

.calendly-inline-widget iframe,
.calendly-badge-widget iframe,
.calendly-overlay iframe {
    display:inline;
    width:100%;
    height:100%
}

.calendly-popup-content {
    position:relative
}

.calendly-popup-content.calendly-mobile {
    -webkit-overflow-scrolling:touch;
    overflow-y:auto
}

.calendly-overlay {
    position:fixed;
    top:0;
    left:0;
    right:0;
    bottom:0;
    overflow:hidden;
    z-index:9999;
    background-color:#a5a5a5;
    background-color:rgba(31,31,31,0.4)
}

.calendly-overlay .calendly-close-overlay {
    position:absolute;
    top:0;
    left:0;
    right:0;
    bottom:0
}

.calendly-overlay .calendly-popup {
    box-sizing:border-box;
    position:absolute;
    top:50%;
    left:50%;
    -webkit-transform:translateY(-50%) translateX(-50%);
    transform:translateY(-50%) translateX(-50%);
    width:80%;
    min-width:900px;
    max-width:1000px;
    height:90%;
    max-height:680px
}

@media (max-width: 975px) {
    .calendly-overlay .calendly-popup {
        position:fixed;
        top:50px;
        left:0;
        right:0;
        bottom:0;
        -webkit-transform:none;
        transform:none;
        width:100%;
        height:auto;
        min-width:0;
        max-height:none
    }
}

.calendly-overlay .calendly-popup .calendly-popup-content {
    height:100%;
}

.calendly-overlay .calendly-popup-close {
    position:absolute;
    top:25px;
    right:25px;
    color:#fff;
    width:19px;
    height:19px;
    cursor:pointer;
    background:url(https://assets.calendly.com/assets/external/close-icon.svg) no-repeat;
    background-size:contain
}

@media (max-width: 975px) {
    .calendly-overlay .calendly-popup-close {
        top:15px;
        right:15px
    }
}

.calendly-badge-widget {
    position:fixed;
    right:20px;
    bottom:15px;
    z-index:9998
}

.calendly-badge-widget .calendly-badge-content {
    display:table-cell;
    width:auto;
    height:45px;
    padding:0 30px;
    border-radius:25px;
    box-shadow:rgba(0,0,0,0.25) 0 2px 5px;
    font-family:sans-serif;
    text-align:center;
    vertical-align:middle;
    font-weight:bold;
    font-size:14px;
    color:#fff;
    cursor:pointer
}

.calendly-badge-widget .calendly-badge-content.calendly-white {
    color:#666a73
}

.calendly-badge-widget .calendly-badge-content span {
    display:block;
    font-size:12px
}

.calendly-spinner {
    position:absolute;
    top:50%;
    left:0;
    right:0;
    -webkit-transform:translateY(-50%);
    transform:translateY(-50%);
    text-align:center;
    z-index:-1
}

.calendly-spinner>div {
    display:inline-block;
    width:18px;
    height:18px;
    background-color:#e1e1e1;
    border-radius:50%;
    vertical-align:middle;
    -webkit-animation:calendly-bouncedelay 1.4s infinite ease-in-out;
    animation:calendly-bouncedelay 1.4s infinite ease-in-out;
    -webkit-animation-fill-mode:both;
    animation-fill-mode:both
}

.calendly-spinner .calendly-bounce1 {
    -webkit-animation-delay:-0.32s;
    animation-delay:-0.32s
}

.calendly-spinner .calendly-bounce2 {
    -webkit-animation-delay:-0.16s;
    animation-delay:-0.16s
}

@-webkit-keyframes calendly-bouncedelay {
    0%,80%,100% {
        -webkit-transform:scale(0);
        transform:scale(0)
    } 
    
    40%{
        -webkit-transform:scale(1);
        transform:scale(1)
    }
}

@keyframes calendly-bouncedelay{ 
    0%,80%,100% {
        -webkit-transform:scale(0);
        transform:scale(0)
    }
    
    40% {
        -webkit-transform:scale(1);
        transform:scale(1)
    }
}</style><style data-emotion="css" data-s=""></style><script src="https://js.stripe.com/dahlia/stripe.js"></script><script type="osano/blocked" defer=""></script></head><body><div data-nosnippet="" class="font-body font-light leading-10">Your pricing page is where deals are won or lost.</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">You can drive website traffic, run ads, and build a great SaaS product. But if your pricing page is unclear, website visitors won’t convert.</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Many SaaS companies struggle to build an effective pricing page. They offer too many plans, display features in a confusing way, and fail to provide clear reasons to upgrade.</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Leading organizations treat pricing as a growth lever. They test it, refine it, and learn from others.</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">This guide breaks down the top 6 SaaS pricing page examples and explains why they work. You can use them as inspiration when building your own pricing page.</p><h2 class="title-h2"><span class="font-bold">TL;DR</span></h2><ul class="list-disc space-y-4 pl-6 text-[1.2rem] lg:!text-[1.4rem]"><li><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Effective SaaS pricing pages have clear headlines, simple plans, feature comparisons, social proof, FAQs, and sometimes, competitor comparisons.</p></li><li><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Top SaaS pricing page examples include Vercel, PostHog, Supabase, Mintlify, Otter.ai, and Linear. They provide clear structures and full transparency, which helps prospects understand and choose the right plan quickly.</p></li><li><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">The best SaaS companies regularly update their pricing pages to reflect new features, customer needs, and market changes.</p></li><li><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Tools like<a href="https://schematichq.com/"> <span class="underline">Schematic</span></a> help you adjust pricing faster, test new pricing models, and enforce in-product access at runtime without hard-coded logic.</p></li></ul><h2 class="title-h2"><span class="font-bold">What Makes a Great SaaS Pricing Page</span></h2><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">The best pricing pages help users decide fast. It removes doubt, builds trust, and shows clear product value. Here are the key elements that separate high-converting SaaS pricing pages from the rest.</p><h3 class="title-h3"><span class="font-bold">Value-Driven Headline and Sub-Headline</span></h3><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">The headline and sub-headline are the first things users see when they visit your pricing page. These should highlight your SaaS’s unique value proposition and not just price.</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Instead of saying "Pricing Plans," show what potential customers get. You can share outcomes like saving time, growing revenue, or scaling workflows.</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">A good headline answers one question: "Why should I care?"</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">If prospects don’t see value right away, they won’t scroll further and sign up.</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Your sub-headline should also hint at your<a href="https://schematichq.com/blog/saas-pricing-models-examples"> <span class="underline">SaaS pricing model</span></a>. For example, mention if you offer a free plan,<a href="https://schematichq.com/blog/seat-based-pricing-101-the-classic-saas-model-that-still-works-sometimes"> <span class="underline">seat-based pricing</span></a>, or usage-based pricing.</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">An effective SaaS pricing page combines value and pricing in one message. This helps users understand your product quickly and decide if your pricing fits their needs.</p><h3 class="title-h3"><span class="font-bold">Simple and Transparent Pricing Plans</span></h3><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Right below your headline and sub-headline is where users expect to see your plans. This section of the pricing page must be clear, structured horizontally, and easy to scan.</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Potential customers should quickly understand multiple pricing plans and know which one is best for their needs.</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Don't go beyond four different pricing tiers to avoid overwhelming visitors. Make sure each plan has a clear purpose and target audience.</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">You should also present plans in a way that’s easy to compare. Use pricing tables that list key features, limitations, and actual costs.</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">If you offer monthly and annual plans, add a simple pricing toggle to avoid confusion and show savings if customers pay annually.</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Many SaaS companies also highlight their recommended plan by marking it as "Most Popular Plan" or "Best Value." This removes decision friction and encourages prospects to sign up faster.</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10"><a href="https://schematichq.com/podcast/the-anatomy-of-a-great-b2b-saas-pricing-page"><span class="font-bold"><span class="italic"><span class="underline">Curious to learn more about the anatomy of a great B2B SaaS pricing page? Check out Schematic's podcast with Jules Boiteux, founder of Roast My Pricing Page.</span></span></span></a></p><h3 class="title-h3"><span class="font-bold">Feature Comparison Table</span></h3><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">A feature comparison table works best for SaaS products with many features. If you try to list everything inside each pricing plan, the page becomes cluttered and hard to read.</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">That's why you should keep your plan tiers simple at the top. Show only the key features.</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Then, use a comparison table, located below, to break down the full details. This allows users to go deeper only if they need to.</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">It keeps the SaaS pricing page clean and organized while still giving full transparency.</p><h3 class="title-h3"><span class="font-bold">Trust Signals and Social Proof</span></h3><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Trust signals and social proof can reduce doubt about your SaaS product. They help potential users feel more confident about choosing your plan.</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Here are some examples you can add to your SaaS pricing page:</p><ul class="list-disc space-y-4 pl-6 text-[1.2rem] lg:!text-[1.4rem]"><li><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Logos</p></li><li><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Customer testimonials</p></li><li><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Star ratings or reviews</p></li><li><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Usage stats (e.g., "Trusted by 10,000+ teams")</p></li><li><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Case study highlights</p></li><li><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Security or compliance badges</p></li></ul><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">You can place these trust signals anywhere on your pricing page. Some SaaS companies add them near the call to action, while others place them close to the pricing plans.</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Choose what fits your pricing page design best.</p><h3 class="title-h3"><span class="font-bold">Frequently Asked Questions</span></h3><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">The FAQ section answers common questions about your SaaS pricing. It removes last-minute doubts before users make an informed decision, whether that's to sign up for a free account or subscribe to a paid plan.</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">You should address the following concerns in the FAQ section:</p><ul class="list-disc space-y-4 pl-6 text-[1.2rem] lg:!text-[1.4rem]"><li><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">What happens after the free trial ends?</p></li><li><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Can users cancel or downgrade anytime?</p></li><li><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Are there any hidden fees or extra charges?</p></li><li><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">How does billing work (monthly vs. yearly pricing)?</p></li><li><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Is support included in all plans?</p></li></ul><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Keep answers short and direct. Focus on the most popular questions that block conversion rates.</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">The best SaaS pricing pages place FAQs near the bottom of the page. By this point, users have already reviewed different plans, so the FAQ section acts as a final checkpoint to clear any remaining doubts.</p><h3 class="title-h3"><span class="font-bold">Competitor Comparison (Optional)</span></h3><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">As a bonus, you can add a competitor comparison section if it fits your value proposition or go-to-market strategy.</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">This is where you clearly position your SaaS product against others. You can show:</p><ul class="list-disc space-y-4 pl-6 text-[1.2rem] lg:!text-[1.4rem]"><li><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Lower pricing than competitors</p></li><li><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">More features at the same price</p></li><li><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Dedicated customer support</p></li></ul><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">The goal is simple: help users understand why your SaaS product is the better choice.</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">This is useful when prospects are actively comparing tools. Instead of leaving your website to do their research, they get the answers right away.</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Keep it honest and direct. Focus on real differences that matter to your target market.</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Not every SaaS company needs this section, but the most effective SaaS pricing pages use it to strengthen their positioning and speed up decisions.</p><h2 class="title-h2"><span class="font-bold">6 SaaS Pricing Page Examples and Why They Work</span></h2><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Here are great examples of effective SaaS pricing pages. We'll also explain what makes them work below.</p><h3 class="title-h3"><span class="font-bold">1. Vercel</span></h3><div class="lg:-mx-[5%]"><img alt="Image" loading="lazy" width="1320" height="700" decoding="async" data-nimg="1" class="rounded-lg" style="color:transparent" srcset="/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fq4fkx4fifpqi%2F2FXXrfXUqZYHRhCInThp3X%2Ffbf104f863a7390f69f532869af0d6e3%2Fimage3.png&w=1920&q=75 1x, /_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fq4fkx4fifpqi%2F2FXXrfXUqZYHRhCInThp3X%2Ffbf104f863a7390f69f532869af0d6e3%2Fimage3.png&w=3840&q=75 2x" src="/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fq4fkx4fifpqi%2F2FXXrfXUqZYHRhCInThp3X%2Ffbf104f863a7390f69f532869af0d6e3%2Fimage3.png&w=3840&q=75"></div><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Source: Vercel.com</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Vercel is a market-leading developer tool. It helps teams build, deploy, and scale web applications faster.</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10"><a href="https://schematichq.com/blog/vercel-pricing"><span class="underline">Vercel pricing</span></a> follows a hybrid model. It combines a seat-based subscription plan with<a href="https://schematichq.com/blog/why-usage-based-billing-is-taking-over-saas"> <span class="underline">usage-based billing</span></a>.</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Its pricing page is relatively straightforward. It follows a simple three-tier structure (Hobby, Pro, and Enterprise) laid out in a clean, side-by-side comparison.</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">The top section is minimal. It highlights key features and includes short descriptions and clear CTAs tailored to different buyer personas. This makes it easy for developers to get started without overthinking.</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">However, Vercel's pricing structure becomes much more complex as you scroll down.</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">What starts simple gradually expands into a long, complex pricing page. It addresses more advanced use cases, features, and enterprise needs. This creates a dual experience: accessible for hobbyists and solo developers, but increasingly comprehensive for enterprise teams.</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">The page is designed to thread the needle between these audiences. It guides users from quick product understanding to deeper evaluation when needed.</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Vercel also leans heavily on transparency. Instead of hiding pricing details, it provides extensive explanations, especially in its FAQ section.</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">The overall structure reflects a clear pricing strategy: start simple, then scale into depth. It supports hobbyists, teams, and enterprise buyers within a single, cohesive pricing page.</p><h4 class="title-h4"><span class="font-bold">Why It Works</span></h4><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Below are the reasons why Vercel’s pricing page works so effectively:</p><ul class="list-disc space-y-4 pl-6 text-[1.2rem] lg:!text-[1.4rem]"><li><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Balances simplicity and depth</p></li><li><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Highly transparent pricing with a detailed FAQ section</p></li><li><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Supports multiple buyer personas (e.g., hobbyists, teams, and enterprise organizations)</p></li><li><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Guides users from self-serve to enterprise</p></li><li><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Confident product positioning</p></li></ul><p class="font-body font-light leading-10"><a href="https://schematichq.com/podcast/a-teardown-of-vercels-pricing-page-and-billing-experience"><span class="font-bold"><span class="italic"><span class="underline">If you want to learn more about Vercel's pricing page and billing experience, you can watch this podcast.</span></span></span></a></p><h3 class="title-h3"><span class="font-bold">2. PostHog</span></h3><div class="lg:-mx-[5%]"><img alt="Image" loading="lazy" width="1320" height="700" decoding="async" data-nimg="1" class="rounded-lg" style="color:transparent" srcset="/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fq4fkx4fifpqi%2FExUyGqyCvsjTT1DNl7jEs%2F215ac000d5ed7df5ae718413d4c99e5b%2Fimage1.png&w=1920&q=75 1x, /_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fq4fkx4fifpqi%2FExUyGqyCvsjTT1DNl7jEs%2F215ac000d5ed7df5ae718413d4c99e5b%2Fimage1.png&w=3840&q=75 2x" src="/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fq4fkx4fifpqi%2FExUyGqyCvsjTT1DNl7jEs%2F215ac000d5ed7df5ae718413d4c99e5b%2Fimage1.png&w=3840&q=75"></div><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Source: PostHog.com</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">PostHog is an all-in-one developer tool that combines product analytics, session replay, and<a href="https://schematichq.com/blog/feature-flag-management"> <span class="underline">feature flags</span></a>, among others.</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">It offers a free plan and a<a href="https://schematichq.com/blog/pay-as-you-go-101-simple-meter-based-pricing"> <span class="underline">pay-as-you-go</span></a> pricing model. It has a clear plan section at the top, which removes decision friction. You start with a free account and pay<a href="https://schematichq.com/blog/overage-pricing-101-base-plans-that-scale-with-usage"> <span class="underline">overage fees</span></a> when you exceed limits.</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Usage-based fees are located just below the pricing plans. This makes it easy to understand costs at a granular level.</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Another key part of the experience is the pricing calculator. You can estimate costs based on usage for each PostHog product.</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Plus, a competitor comparison table shows why PostHog is the best choice.</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Every aspect of<a href="https://schematichq.com/podcast/posthogs-pricing-page-pricing-as-an-extension-of-brand"> <span class="underline">PostHog's pricing page is an extension of its brand</span></a>. It reflects their unique tone, humor, and transparency.</p><h4 class="title-h4"><span class="font-bold">Why It Works</span></h4><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Here's what makes PostHog's pricing page effective:</p><ul class="list-disc space-y-4 pl-6 text-[1.2rem] lg:!text-[1.4rem]"><li><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Fun, unique pricing experience due to its e-commerce checkout page design</p></li><li><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Fully transparent pricing with calculators to estimate costs</p></li><li><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Removes decision friction</p></li><li><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Reflects PostHog’s humorous tone and personality</p></li><li><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Includes everything you need on a pricing page (e.g., tiers, FAQ, proposition value, social proof, competitor comparison, add-ons)</p></li></ul><h3 class="title-h3"><span class="font-bold">3. It starts with a tiered pricing structure: Free, Pro, Team, and Enterprise.</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Each tier highlights key features, such as database size, file storage, and monthly active users (MAU). This layout makes it easy to scan different pricing plans and get started.</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">As you scroll down, the pricing page offers depth. It introduces usage-based pricing components like compute size, storage, and add-ons. Many features link to the documentation page for additional information.</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">The FAQ section is another effective element that guides users through pricing decisions.</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Supabase follows a unique pricing strategy when it comes to compute. Instead of charging based only on utilization, it ties pricing to instance size and capacity. This makes costs more predictable.</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Despite the complexity, Supabase's pricing page feels simple and approachable for both hobbyists and enterprises.</p><h4 class="title-h4"><span class="font-bold">Why It Works</span></h4><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Supabase's pricing page is a<a href="https://schematichq.com/podcast/supabases-pricing-page-a-masterclass-in-predictable-pricing"> <span class="underline">masterclass in predictable pricing</span></a> due to the following reasons:</p><ul class="list-disc space-y-4 pl-6 text-[1.2rem] lg:!text-[1.4rem]"><li><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Instance-based pricing with transparent overage policies</p></li><li><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Robust cost control mechanisms</p></li><li><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Linked docs and a detailed FAQ section</p></li><li><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Transparent costs build long-term loyalty</p></li><li><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Pricing as part of the user experience with the company</p></li></ul><h3 class="title-h3"><span class="font-bold">4. Otter.ai</span></h3><div class="lg:-mx-[5%]"><img alt="Image" loading="lazy" width="1320" height="700" decoding="async" data-nimg="1" class="rounded-lg" style="color:transparent" srcset="/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fq4fkx4fifpqi%2F518S1Rg4xIZRmYMkyrEtrC%2F31499f51514468a4080e2c7dbb90988f%2Fimage6.png&w=1920&q=75 1x, /_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fq4fkx4fifpqi%2F518S1Rg4xIZRmYMkyrEtrC%2F31499f51514468a4080e2c7dbb90988f%2Fimage6.png&w=3840&q=75 2x" src="/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fq4fkx4fifpqi%2F518S1Rg4xIZRmYMkyrEtrC%2F31499f51514468a4080e2c7dbb90988f%2Fimage6.png&w=3840&q=75"></div><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Source: Otter.ai</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Otter.ai is an AI notetaker that transcribes meetings into knowledge bases, insights, notes, and summaries.</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Otter.ai uses a tiered, per-user subscription pricing model that supports both<a href="https://schematichq.com/blog/plg-onboarding-the-first-15-minutes-determine-your-growth-curve"> <span class="underline">product-led growth</span></a> and sales-led motions.</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Its pricing page is clean and structured for quick comparison. A pricing toggle at the top lets you switch between monthly and annual billing.</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Below, you'll see the four main pricing plans: Basic (free), Pro, Business, and Enterprise. Each one highlights different features and key limitations, like transcription minutes and meeting duration.</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Pricing tiers and features are displayed side by side in increasing value. The page guides users to see the "next level" as the natural upgrade path. For example, small organizations can start with Basic, then switch to Pro and Business as their business needs expand.</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">At the bottom, the short and sweet FAQ section answers common questions about Otter.ai's pricing. It reduces decision friction and handles objections effectively.</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10"><a href="https://schematichq.com/podcast/how-naveen-mohan-and-otter-ai-drive-pricing-packaging-and-monetization"><span class="font-bold"><span class="italic"><span class="underline">Want to learn more about how Otter.ai prices and packages its SaaS product? You can check this podcast where Schematic talks to Naveen Mohan, who leads monetization efforts at Otter.ai.</span></span></span></a></p><h4 class="title-h4"><span class="font-bold">Why It Works</span></h4><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Here’s why Otter.ai’s pricing page works so well:</p><ul class="list-disc space-y-4 pl-6 text-[1.2rem] lg:!text-[1.4rem]"><li><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Keeps pricing simple and easy to understand</p></li><li><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Makes upgrades feel natural as usage grows</p></li><li><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Transparent feature-by-feature comparison and limits</p></li><li><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Reduces decision friction</p></li><li><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Uses a pricing toggle to show savings clearly</p></li></ul><h3 class="title-h3"><span class="font-bold">6. Linear</span></h3><div class="lg:-mx-[5%]"><img alt="Image" loading="lazy" width="1320" height="700" decoding="async" data-nimg="1" class="rounded-lg" style="color:transparent" srcset="/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fq4fkx4fifpqi%2F2ZszS1R9Skp7l0RfFV6XQF%2F295c9e2243bf98e21fbdeac79523f735%2Fimage7.png&w=1920&q=75 1x, /_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fq4fkx4fifpqi%2F2ZszS1R9Skp7l0RfFV6XQF%2F295c9e2243bf98e21fbdeac79523f735%2Fimage7.png&w=3840&q=75 2x" src="/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fq4fkx4fifpqi%2F2ZszS1R9Skp7l0RfFV6XQF%2F295c9e2243bf98e21fbdeac79523f735%2Fimage7.png&w=3840&q=75"></div><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Source: Linear.app</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Linear is a product development system and issue tracking tool. It's designed for speed and collaboration between humans and AI agents.</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">It uses a seat-based subscription model. A free plan is designed for hobbyists and early-stage startups, while three higher tiers cater to scaling businesses.</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10"><a href="https://schematichq.com/podcast/linear-pricing-page-and-billing-ux-teardown-what-saas-founders-can-learn"><span class="underline">Linear's pricing page</span></a> is very simple. You instantly know what you’re getting, what you’ll pay, and how to upgrade to a custom plan if needed.</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Four subscription packages are shown side by side, and it's easy to get started with the first three tiers. There is also a clear "Contact Sales Team" CTA as businesses grow. This supports both self-serve and sales-led motion.</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Each tier includes just enough detail upfront. Potential buyers can quickly understand the differences and choose the right plan without scrolling. The page signals early that this is not a complex decision-making process.</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">A comprehensive feature breakdown is located below the tiers to help users compare different plans.</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">The page also includes social proof with customer logos and links to customer stories.</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">One thing to note is that Linear's pricing page lacks an FAQ section. Still, it makes sense since their pricing is simple enough.</p><h4 class="title-h4"><span class="font-bold">Why It Works</span></h4><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Linear's pricing page works due to several reasons:</p><ul class="list-disc space-y-4 pl-6 text-[1.2rem] lg:!text-[1.4rem]"><li><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Keeps pricing simple and easy to understand</p></li><li><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Intuitive user experience</p></li><li><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Low barrier to entry</p></li><li><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Obvious upgrade paths between tiers</p></li><li><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Introduces complexity gradually</p></li></ul><h2 class="title-h2"><span class="font-bold">Common Mistakes to Avoid in SaaS Pricing Pages</span></h2><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Below are the common mistakes you should avoid when building your SaaS pricing page.</p><ul class="list-disc space-y-4 pl-6 text-[1.2rem] lg:!text-[1.4rem]"><li><p class="font-body font-light leading-10"><span class="font-bold">Too many plans</span> - Excessive pricing options create confusion and slow purchasing decisions. Stick to no more than four tiers. Each plan should cater to a specific target audience.</p></li><li><p class="font-body font-light leading-10"><span class="font-bold">Feature overload</span> - Listing too many unique features overwhelms prospects. They stop reading and miss key differences. Focus on the most important features. Discuss details in a comparison table below the main tiers.</p></li><li><p class="font-body font-light leading-10"><span class="font-bold">Hidden pricing information</span> - Hiding costs builds distrust. Users want clarity upfront. Be transparent about costs, limits, and add-ons. Avoid forcing users to contact sales teams just to see pricing.</p></li><li><p class="font-body font-light leading-10"><span class="font-bold">Poor mobile experience</span> - Around<a href="https://www.statista.com/statistics/277125/share-of-website-traffic-coming-from-mobile-devices/"> <span class="underline">62% of website traffic</span></a> comes from mobile devices. If your pricing page is hard to read or scroll on mobile, potential customers will leave. Use responsive design and keep layouts simple.</p></li><li><p class="font-body font-light leading-10"><span class="font-bold">Lack of social proof</span> - Without social proof, prospects hesitate to trust your product. Add logos, testimonials, or usage stats. This reassures users that others already use and value your SaaS product.</p></li><li><p class="font-body font-light leading-10"><span class="font-bold">Static pricing</span> - Pricing should not stay the same forever. If you never update it, you miss growth opportunities. Review and adjust pricing based on product value, market changes, and user behavior.</p></li></ul><h2 class="title-h2"><span class="font-bold">Why Do You Need to Change SaaS Pricing?</span></h2><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">You need to update your SaaS pricing because your product, customers, and market evolve constantly.</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">As your product improves, your pricing should reflect that added value. For example, new features or better performance give you a reason to adjust pricing. If you keep the same pricing, you may undercharge and lose revenue.</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Your target customers may also change. You might move from small teams to larger companies. This shift requires different plans, limits, and pricing structures.</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Market conditions also play a role. Competitors change pricing, and customer expectations shift over time.</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Pricing is not a one-time decision. It should evolve as your business grows and your SaaS product becomes more valuable.</p><h2 class="title-h2"><span class="font-bold">Continuously Iterate on Pricing With Schematic</span></h2><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Your SaaS pricing page will eventually need an update. The question is whether that change takes days or several months.</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10"><a href="https://schematichq.com/"><span class="underline">Schematic</span></a> helps you launch any pricing model fast without rebuilding your billing stack. It's the monetization operating system that sits between your SaaS application and Stripe.</p><div class="lg:-mx-[5%]"><img alt="Image" loading="lazy" width="1320" height="700" decoding="async" data-nimg="1" class="rounded-lg" style="color:transparent" srcset="/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fq4fkx4fifpqi%2F7xceC82UxcCHsRa7Nf1PKG%2Feadb76e4d1a47d7e579b8ebaffd93df1%2FSchematic.png&w=1920&q=75 1x, /_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fq4fkx4fifpqi%2F7xceC82UxcCHsRa7Nf1PKG%2Feadb76e4d1a47d7e579b8ebaffd93df1%2FSchematic.png&w=3840&q=75 2x" src="/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fq4fkx4fifpqi%2F7xceC82UxcCHsRa7Nf1PKG%2Feadb76e4d1a47d7e579b8ebaffd93df1%2FSchematic.png&w=3840&q=75"></div><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">It serves as the system of record for your product catalog. Plans,<a href="https://schematichq.com/blog/software-entitlements"> <span class="underline">software entitlements</span></a>, limits, trials, credits, add-ons, and exceptions live in one place.</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">So instead of hard-coding pricing logic, you can update<a href="https://schematichq.com/blog/how-to-operate-pricing-and-packaging"> <span class="underline">packaging and pricing</span></a> in real time. Roll out pricing tests and feature access instantly without waiting on developers.</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Engineering stops writing and maintaining billing code. Schematic evaluates and enforces access in-product at runtime.</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">Product and GTM teams can continuously iterate on monetization.</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10"><a href="https://schematichq.com/"><span class="font-bold"><span class="italic"><span class="underline">Book a demo to get started!</span></span></span></a></p><h2 class="title-h2"><span class="font-bold">FAQs About SaaS Pricing Page Examples</span></h2><h3 class="title-h3"><span class="font-bold">What are the key elements of an effective SaaS pricing page?</span></h3><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">An effective SaaS pricing page includes a value-driven headline, clear pricing plans, feature comparisons, social proof, and an FAQ section. A competitor comparison is a bonus element that can help potential customers evaluate options without leaving your SaaS website.</p><h3 class="title-h3"><span class="font-bold">What is the best layout for a SaaS pricing page?</span></h3><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">The best SaaS pricing page layout is simple and structured. Start with a value-driven headline, followed by three to four pricing tiers organized horizontally. Use a toggle for monthly and annual pricing if applicable. Add a feature comparison below for more detail. Include social proof throughout the page to build trust. Finally, place FAQs at the bottom. This layout helps users scan, compare, and decide faster.</p><h3 class="title-h3"><span class="font-bold">Why do you need to update your SaaS pricing page?</span></h3><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">You need to update your pricing page because your product, paying customers, and pricing strategy change over time. As you add new features or target new segments, your current pricing may no longer fit. If you don’t update it, you risk underpricing or slowing growth. Regular updates keep your pricing aligned with value and business goals.</p><p class="font-body font-light leading-10">
